what_it_does: This file stores a minimal YAML-frontmatter metadata record that links an attachment (att-49437b9e) to a channel board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
file body after frontmatter is empty
standouts: The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-57a213af
The attachment is typed as 'board_items' for the dragnet-development channel
It is a timestamped, ID-prefixed attachment record
concerns: No content hash or size is present to verify the attachment
The relationship semantics between attachment and board item are implicit
notes: A bare channel-memory attachment metadata stub that ties att-49437b9e to board item item-57a213af in dragnet-development.
questions: What payload or content does this attachment represent?
Who added this attachment and why?
What is the exact lifecycle/role of a 'board_items' attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment registry entry and it does exactly that: it records an attachment ID, type, target board item, and creation time.
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a durable, discoverable record that a board item in the dragnet-development channel has an associated attachment.
contribution: It contributes a persistent, timestamped attachment-to-board-item reference for the dragnet-development channel, supporting lookup and provenance of board item attachments.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel-memory attachment record named att-49437b9e in dragnet-development that targets board item item-57a213af with type board_items and an ISO timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register and timestamp an attachment association between a channel artifact and a specific board item.
